description: NTS_RM_LLN Algorithm Input Example - Response Matrix with Linear Lattice Nodalization for 2D Neutron Transport

metadata.algorithm: Response Matrix (RM) + Linear Lattice Nodalization (LLN) + Discrete Ordinates (SN)
metadata.method_type: Response Matrix Method with Linear Lattice Nodalization
metadata.file_type: Plain text input (converted to JSON for reference)
metadata.variant: Alternative RM implementation focusing on lattice discretization

program_overview.name: NTS_RM_LLN.c
program_overview.purpose: Solves 2D neutron transport equation using Response Matrix method with Linear Lattice Nodalization
program_overview.method_description: Combines Response Matrix formulation with linear lattice nodalization for accurate spatial discretization
program_overview.advantages: Fast convergence, excellent accuracy, stable with complex geometries
program_overview.computational_cost: Similar to RM_CN but with different nodalization strategy
program_overview.input_parameters: Identical to NTS_DD, NTS_LD, NTS_RM_CN (11 parameters)
program_overview.output_format: JSON with STATUS, ITER, CPU, MFLUX, MFLOW, XFLOW, YFLOW

nodalization_approach.RM_LLN: Linear Lattice Nodalization - refined spatial grid at nodes

spatial_accuracy.RM_LLN: O(h²) spatial accuracy with refined lattice

convergence_pattern.RM_LLN: Fast (5-15 iterations, slightly more iterations but different physics)

applicability.RM_LLN: Better for lattice-based reactor cores, heterogeneous assemblies

computational_cost.RM_LLN: Similar complexity with focus on nodal refinement

example_1_simple_lattice.name: Simple 2×2 Lattice - RM_LLN Method
example_1_simple_lattice.description: Simple domain 20×20 cm discretized as 2×2 lattice, one material, uniform sources
example_1_simple_lattice.physics: Tests RM_LLN with simple lattice geometry - optimal for LLN method

json_format.accuracy_level: High - RM_LLN excels with lattice problems

expected_output.convergence_pattern: Fast - RM_LLN converges quickly with lattice-based input
expected_output.accuracy: High, particularly suited to assembly lattices

example_2_reactor_assembly.name: Fuel Assembly with Control Rod Channels
example_2_reactor_assembly.description: 3×3 assembly lattice with central control rod region - typical BWR/PWR assembly
example_2_reactor_assembly.physics: RM_LLN designed for this: multiple material zones in assembly geometry

json_format.geometry: 3×3 assembly with control rod in center

example_3_core_fragment.name: Small Core Fragment (2×2 Assemblies)
example_3_core_fragment.description: 4×4 lattice representing core quadrant with multiple assembly types
example_3_core_fragment.physics: RM_LLN optimal: lattice structure with realistic heterogeneity

json_format.note: LLN approach naturally handles lattice patterns

example_4_fine_lattice_study.name: Fine Lattice (5×5) - Detailed Analysis
example_4_fine_lattice_study.description: 5×5 lattice with varying cross-sections for detailed physics studies
example_4_fine_lattice_study.physics: LLN excellent for detailed nodal problems

parameter_definitions.note: Input parameters identical to all three previous methods (11 parameters total)

N.name: Quadrature Order
N.recommendation: 4-6 for RM_LLN (4 typical for assembly problems)

NZ.name: Number of Material Zones
NZ.recommendation: 1-3 for typical assembly, up to 10 for core fragments

NR_X, XDOM.name: X Domain Discretization
NR_X, XDOM.recommendation: Match lattice pitch (typically 4-6 nodes per region)

NR_Y, YDOM.name: Y Domain Discretization
NR_Y, YDOM.recommendation: Match lattice pitch (typically 4-6 nodes per region)

QMAP.name: Source Map
QMAP.note: Can model distributed neutron sources in assembly

method_explanation.linear_lattice_nodalization: Discretizes domain into uniform lattice with refined nodal grid at intersections
method_explanation.lattice_structure: Natural representation of reactor assemblies (typical 5.5 cm pitch)
method_explanation.nodal_coupling: Couples angular fluxes at lattice boundaries for conservation
method_explanation.response_matrix: Precomputed transfer operators for inter-lattice communication
method_explanation.advantages: Perfect match for assembly-based problems, lattice calculations

output_fields.MFLUX: Scalar flux matrix at lattice nodes

assembly_analysis.description: Single fuel assembly with control rods

lattice_physics.example: Infinite lattice unit cell with boundary conditions

arbitrary_2d_problems.note: Use DD or LD instead - RM_LLN optimized for lattices

performance_characteristics.cpu_time: Low to Medium (few iterations × matrix ops)
performance_characteristics.scalability: Good up to 10×10 lattices on standard hardware
